meta.name: Daily Reflection Tree
meta.description: End-of-day deterministic reflection tool. No LLM at runtime.

nodes.text: Good evening. No performance review here — just a few minutes to look at your day honestly. Let's begin.
Pick one word that captures how today felt.
options: Productive
Tough
Mixed
Frustrating
answer=Productive|Mixed:A1_Q1_HIGH
answer=Tough|Frustrating:A1_Q1_LOW

nodes.text: You felt "{A1_OPEN.answer}" today. When something went well, what drove it?
options: I prepared and it paid off
I adapted when the plan broke
My team came through
Honestly, it just worked out

nodes.text: You felt "{A1_OPEN.answer}" today. When things got hard, what was your first move?
options: I looked for what I could still control
I waited to see how others would handle it
I pushed through on my own
I got stuck and couldn't move forward
answer=I prepared and it paid off|I adapted when the plan broke:A1_Q2_INT
answer=My team came through|Honestly, it just worked out:A1_Q2_EXT
answer=I looked for what I could still control|I pushed through on my own:A1_Q2_INT
answer=I waited to see how others would handle it|I got stuck and couldn't move forward:A1_Q2_EXT

nodes.text: Think of one moment today where you had a choice about how to respond. What did you actually do?
options: I made a deliberate call, even under pressure
I defaulted to habit — it worked out
I reacted and only later realized I had options
I handed it off — it wasn't mine to solve

nodes.text: When today's obstacles showed up, how much of what happened felt within your influence?
options: More than I first thought — I did have some control
Very little — the situation was genuinely out of my hands
Hard to say — it was tangled
None. I was just reacting to everyone else
answer=I made a deliberate call, even under pressure|I defaulted to habit — it worked out:A1_R_INT
answer=I reacted and only later realized I had options|I handed it off — it wasn't mine to solve:A1_R_MIXED
answer=More than I first thought — I did have some control|Hard to say — it was tangled:A1_R_MIXED
answer=Very little — the situation was genuinely out of my hands|None. I was just reacting to everyone else:A1_R_EXT

nodes.text: You stayed in the driver's seat today — not because things were easy, but because you kept looking for what you could do. That's not a small thing. Most people stop there and call it the situation's fault.
Today was somewhere in the middle — you had some control, maybe more than felt obvious in the moment. The awareness that you had options, even if you didn't fully take them, is worth holding onto.

Today felt like things were happening to you, not with you. That's real — some days are like that. But somewhere in there, you made at least one call. What was it? You don't have to answer now. Just sit with it for a second.
Now let's shift. Less about how you handled things — more about what you gave.
Think about the interactions you had today. Which of these feels truest?
options: I mostly gave — helped, supported, showed up
I mostly received — I needed things from others
It was balanced
I kept to myself — not much interaction
answer=I mostly gave — helped, supported, showed up|It was balanced:A2_Q1_CONTRIB
answer=I mostly received — I needed things from others|I kept to myself — not much interaction:A2_Q1_ENTITLE

nodes.text: Was there a moment today where you helped someone with something that wasn't strictly your job?
options: Yes — I stepped in without being asked
Yes — but only after I was asked
No — I had enough on my plate
No — I didn't see an opportunity

nodes.text: Was there a moment today where you felt the effort you put in wasn't being recognized?
options: Yes — I deserved more acknowledgment than I got
A little — but I let it go
Not really — I wasn't focused on that
No — recognition wasn't on my mind today
answer=Yes — I stepped in without being asked|Yes — but only after I was asked:A2_Q2_CONTRIB
answer=No — I had enough on my plate|No — I didn't see an opportunity:A2_Q2_MIXED
answer=Yes — I deserved more acknowledgment than I got|A little — but I let it go:A2_Q2_ENTITLE
answer=Not really — I wasn't focused on that|No — recognition wasn't on my mind today:A2_Q2_MIXED

nodes.text: When you gave today — time, effort, knowledge — did it feel like a drain or like the right thing to do?
options: Right thing — I didn't think twice
A bit of both, but I'm glad I did it
Honestly a drain — I gave more than I had
I'm not sure it mattered to them

nodes.text: When you felt overlooked or undervalued today, what did that feeling make you want to do?
options: Pull back — why give more if it goes unnoticed?
Work harder to make my contribution visible
Talk to someone about it
Let it go and refocus

nodes.text: If you had given 10% more to a colleague or task today — with no expectation of recognition — would that have changed the outcome for them?
options: Probably yes — there was room to give more
Maybe — hard to say
No — I was already at my limit
I don't think it would have mattered
answer=Right thing — I didn't think twice|A bit of both, but I'm glad I did it:A2_R_CONTRIB
answer=Honestly a drain — I gave more than I had|I'm not sure it mattered to them:A2_R_MIXED
answer=Pull back — why give more if it goes unnoticed?|Work harder to make my contribution visible:A2_R_ENTITLE
answer=Talk to someone about it|Let it go and refocus:A2_R_MIXED
answer=Probably yes — there was room to give more|Maybe — hard to say:A2_R_MIXED
answer=No — I was already at my limit|I don't think it would have mattered:A2_R_ENTITLE

nodes.text: You gave today without making it transactional. That's not a personality trait — it's a practice. Most people don't even notice they're keeping score until they're deep in it.

Today was somewhere between giving and keeping score. Both exist in most people — the question is which one you want to feed tomorrow. What would it look like to give 10% more, with zero expectation of return?

Part of today was spent tracking what you were getting — recognition, credit, fairness. That's human. But it's also a slow leak. The people who feel most fulfilled at work tend to have stopped that accounting. Not because they're selfless — because they found it more useful.
One last lens. Let's zoom out — from you, to everyone around you.
When you think about today's biggest challenge, whose experience comes to mind first?
options: Mine — it was my problem to carry
My team's — we were all in it
A specific colleague who had it harder
The person or customer we're ultimately serving
answer=Mine — it was my problem to carry:A3_Q1_SELF
answer=My team's — we were all in it:A3_Q1_TEAM
answer=A specific colleague who had it harder|The person or customer we're ultimately serving:A3_Q1_OTHER

nodes.text: Did anyone around you today seem like they were struggling — even quietly?
options: Yes — I noticed but didn't act on it
Yes — and I checked in
I wasn't in a position to notice
No — people seemed fine

nodes.text: When you thought about how the team was doing today, what was your instinct?
options: We needed to pull together — I tried to help that happen
I noticed the gaps but stayed focused on my own work
I was part of the problem, honestly
The team was fine — I worried about my own piece

nodes.text: When you thought about that colleague or customer today, what did you actually do with that awareness?
options: I acted on it — did something to help
I thought about it but didn't act
I mentioned it to someone else
Nothing — I noted it and moved on
answer=Yes — and I checked in:A3_Q2_EXPAND
answer=Yes — I noticed but didn't act on it|I wasn't in a position to notice|No — people seemed fine:A3_Q2_NARROW
answer=We needed to pull together — I tried to help that happen:A3_Q2_EXPAND
answer=I noticed the gaps but stayed focused on my own work|I was part of the problem, honestly|The team was fine — I worried about my own piece:A3_Q2_NARROW
answer=I acted on it — did something to help|I mentioned it to someone else:A3_Q2_EXPAND
answer=I thought about it but didn't act|Nothing — I noted it and moved on:A3_Q2_NARROW

nodes.text: When you oriented toward someone else's experience today, did it make your own problems feel different?
options: Yes — it gave me perspective
Not really — I was too in my own head
I hadn't thought about it that way
It actually made things heavier — I took on too much

nodes.text: Is there one person in your world right now — team, family, colleague — who might need something from you tomorrow?
options: Yes — I know exactly who
Probably — I'd have to think
Maybe, but I can't add more right now
Not that I can think of
answer=Yes — it gave me perspective|I hadn't thought about it that way:A3_R_ALTO
answer=Not really — I was too in my own head|It actually made things heavier — I took on too much:A3_R_MIXED
answer=Yes — I know exactly who|Probably — I'd have to think:A3_R_MIXED
answer=Maybe, but I can't add more right now|Not that I can think of:A3_R_SELF

nodes.text: You widened the frame today. That's rarer than it sounds. Most people move through the day in a tunnel — their tasks, their stress, their goals. You caught a glimpse of someone else's experience and let it matter. That's the beginning of something.

There was a flicker today — a moment where someone else's reality entered your field of view. You didn't fully act on it, and that's okay. Awareness precedes action. The question is whether tomorrow you let that flicker become something.

Today the lens was close — mostly you, your challenges, your frame. That's not a flaw, it's a starting point. But the most reliable source of meaning at work isn't internal. It lives in what you contribute to something beyond yourself. Worth experimenting with tomorrow.
That's it. Same time tomorrow.

axis1.internal: You owned your responses today — even when things got hard.
axis1.external: Today felt like it happened to you. Somewhere in it, there was a choice. Find it.
axis1.mixed: You had more agency than the day made obvious. It's worth noticing.

axis2.contribution: You gave without keeping score. That compounds.
axis2.entitlement: Part of today was spent tracking what you were owed. Tomorrow, try giving first.
axis2.mixed: You balanced giving and receiving. See if you can tip it slightly toward giving tomorrow.

axis3.altrocentric: Your concern reached beyond yourself today. That's where meaning lives.
axis3.collective: You thought about the team. Keep that radius wide.
axis3.self: The frame was mostly you today. Tomorrow, let one other person's reality fully matter to you.

closing.all_positive: Three for three. Whatever you did today — repeat it deliberately tomorrow.
closing.mixed: Not a bad day. A real one. The reflection is the practice.
closing.all_negative: Hard day. The fact that you're here doing this — looking at it honestly — is the whole point.
